rulings.Case Title: VIP SPORTS MARKETING, INC. VS. THOMAS LYHNE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 12, 2010, line 3, PLAINTIFF VIP SPORTS MARKETING, INC.'S (Amended) Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Grant motion to amend. Motion to continue trial, if sought, must be made in Presiding Judge's department, per SFLR 6.0B. Discovery motions to be made to the Discovery Commissioner (SFLR 10.0A). =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: REGULO SIERRA VS. STEVEN KREMER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 12, 2010, line 5. THIS IS A LATE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT STEVEN KREMER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer to complaint sustained without leave to amend. Res judicata bars the filing of this complaint which is identical to an earlier complaint filed by plaintiff which was dismissed with prejudice. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: REGULO SIERRA VS. STEVEN KREMER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 12, 2010, line 5. THIS IS A LATE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT STEVEN KREMER MOTION TO STRIKE IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT IN LIGHT OF RULING ON DEMURRER. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LEW JENKINS VS. THE PROPERTIES SCIENCES GROUP, INC
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 12, 2010, line 6. DEFENDANT THE PROPERTIES SCIENCES GROUP, INC, BERNARD DUER, PAUL CHANDLER MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Grant motion to strike, with leave to amend.=(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LEW JENKINS VS. THE PROPERTIES SCIENCES GROUP, INC
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 12, 2010, line 6. DEFENDANTS THE PROPERTIES SCIENCES GROUP, INC, BERNARD DUER, PAUL CHANDLER DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustain demurrer to second cause of action with leave to amend to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action for negligent mispresentation. Sustain demurrer to third cause of action with leave to amend to plead with more specificity which prong(s) of UCL apply, and with factual allegations. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CARMEN ROSA LAU et al VS. JORGE ALUY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 12, 2010, line 7. PLAINTIFFS CARMEN LAU, ROSA ALUY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Court did not receive Exhibits A and B to declaration of Carmen Rosa Aluy Lau. If plaintiff can come to court and prove that the served copy of the motions included these exhibits, leave will be given to bring the exhibits to the hearing to correct the clerical error of not filing them, and motion will be granted. Otherwise, deny motion; no opposition filed but exhibits referred to in declaration not attached. =(302/MJM)
